marginally supportive of our sticky
inflation theme it is not entirely clear

markets is the uncertainty surrounding
the size sequence and scope of President

picks suggest tariffs and mass
deportations will be a core feature of

program and speaking of their fake QT
program arguably the most important part

the discussion surrounding the balance

the debt selling moratorium and the
potential for a significant TGA spin
down early in the year and how that some
of that TGA spin down May wind up on the
fed's balance sheet in the form of an
increasing RRP which would obviously
offset that TGA spend down from a

recall that they did a a five basis
point technical adjustment to the
overnight reverse hoo facility rate uh

fed's balance sheet is less attractive
to money market fund participants in the

punts on on the Deb selling moratorium
which they very well may I mean you know
they're they're going to use budget

into the future the current moratorium

delay uh any uh TJ spin down uh
associated with you know kind of

already there you know associated with
the you know the US Treasury running out
of borrowing Authority you know so this
is a real you know a lot of moving Parts
if you can't tell and this is only one
narrow slope of the things that matter
to asset markets you're talking about
trying to get asset markets right you
got to get growth right you got to get
inflation right you gotta get monetary
policy right you gotta get fiscal policy
right you gotta get the positioning
cycle right you know corporate profit
Cycles is really instrumental as well
and then obviously you got to get
liquidity right and I would argue
liquidity kind of is the outcrop of
growth inflation uh monetary fiscal
policy uh and corporate profits you know
so that's kind of the outcrop of that
but you got to get all the stuff right
if you want to have you know
consistently stay on the right out of
Market risk the alternative which is the
more humble more thoughtful in my
opinion way that we've chosen here at 42
macro is is yes we're always at all
times trying to forecast all of that
stuff but the the the the the signals
that matter most that that that signals
that actually matter to our asset
allocation and portfolio construction
recommendations that we use to keep our
clients on the right side of Market risk
at all times are our quantitative risk
management signaling our quantitative
risk management signaling process
processes like kiss and Dr Mo you know
they are ad mission that there's no way
possible for someone who's even as
talented as I am at predicting all this
stuff is going to constantly predict all
that stuff correctly you know so if
you're less talented than I am at
predicting that stuff you know you have
to accept the fact that you're probably
going to have even more tracking error
with respect to your foreca forecast
error with respect to your projections
for all those different cycles that
impact asset markets than even I would
have and I've already decided to listen
to the market and let the market dictate
our asset allocation and portfolio
construction so uh you know we'll wrap
it up there and as you sort of you know
assuming here here in America even if
you're not in America over the next few
days I want you to be really critically
thinking about what I just said about
you know incorporating more humility
into your investment process accepting
the fact that you are not smart enough
to predict all those cycles that we just
talked about consistently and accurately
over the course of time what you can do
however is listen to find a ways to
incorporate more quantitative
riskmanagement signaling into your
process so that you can listen to the
market and participate in Raging Bull
markets like 42 macro clients have for
the past couple of years or you can get
to the sideline Andor short heading into
raging bare markets like 42 macro
clients did back in 2022 there's a
reason we have a phenomenal track record
over the last three and a half plus
years is because we have the humility to
accept the fact that we can't predict
all that stuff consistently and
accurately and that the market is
smarter than us and that we should be
following the market and participating
in what the market is trying to price in
at any given juncture that's a much
better outcome than you know trying to
tell the market what to do based on your
fundamental predictions which may or may
not be as you know deeply researched as
ours are here at 42 macro and everyone
in the world knows how deeply researched
our fundamental research views are so
